alert: ALERT: Login latency p99 > 8s. Auth service CPU at 99%. Users cannot sign in.
root_cause: Hot loop in JWT validation code causing CPU-bound infinite loop in auth-service
resolution_steps: Check auth-service CPU metrics and confirm at 99%
Restart auth-service to clear runaway thread
Deploy hotfix for JWT validation logic

alert: ALERT: Write operations failing. postgres-db throwing ENOSPC errors. Order creation down 100%.
root_cause: PostgreSQL WAL (write-ahead log) grew unbounded after auto-vacuum was disabled; disk hit 100% capacity
resolution_steps: Confirm disk usage via DB query on postgres-db
Clean up WAL logs and free disk space

alert: ALERT: Cache miss rate 100%. Session lookups failing. All endpoints 3x slower.
root_cause: Network interface configuration change on redis-cache host causing TCP unreachability from app tier
resolution_steps: Check redis-cache health status (shows DOWN despite process running)
Verify network connectivity from app tier to redis host

alert: ALERT: Order service unreachable. 100% error rate on /checkout endpoint. Revenue impact.
root_cause: Memory leak in order aggregation query loading entire order history into memory instead of streaming; JVM OOM

alert: ALERT: SSL handshake failures. 100% of HTTPS requests rejected. Certificate error.
root_cause: TLS certificate for api-gateway expired; cert renewal cron job failed silently 30 days ago due to permissions issue
resolution_steps: Check certificate expiration in api-gateway logs
Rollback api-gateway to previous cert configuration

alert: ALERT: Transaction timeouts across all services. DB response time 30s+. All writes queued.
root_cause: Two concurrent transactions (order-service and notification-service) entered classical deadlock; PostgreSQL deadlock detector killing transactions every 30s
resolution_steps: Run DB query on postgres-db to inspect pg_locks
Kill deadlocked transaction or restart service

alert: ALERT: Email/SMS notifications not sending. notification-service restarting every 30s.
root_cause: Bad startup configuration (missing Twilio API key environment variable) causing NullPointerException in CrashLoopBackOff

alert: ALERT: 429 Too Many Requests errors from upstream. API gateway returning errors to all clients.
root_cause: Background job synced product catalog triggering 50,000 API calls in 10 minutes, hitting upstream rate limit

alert: ALERT: Orders failing silently. No 500s but completion rate dropped to 12%. Auth latency 2s.
root_cause: Configuration drift in order-service where feature flag config fell out of sync with database schema; missing schema field causes silent null writes
red_herrings: auth-service (elevated latency from connection queue backup)
resolution_steps: Check order-service logs for null writes
Run DB query to confirm null writes in orders table
Rollback order-service deployment to restore config sync

alert: ALERT: All services degraded. Response times 10x normal. order-service CPU 90%.
root_cause: Missing database index on orders table (50M rows) causes full table scan on every checkout request taking 8 seconds instead of 4ms
red_herrings: order-service (high CPU from retrying slow queries)
resolution_steps: Check postgres-db query metrics for seq_scan with 50M rows examined
Run DB query to identify missing index

alert: ALERT: Gradual service degradation over 2 hours. notification-service GC pauses 10s+.
root_cause: Memory leak in email template renderer holding references to rendered HTML strings in static cache without eviction; heap grew from 400MB to 3.8GB
resolution_steps: Check notification-service logs for 'GC pause > 10000ms' and 'Old generation 98% full'

alert: ALERT: Login requests hanging indefinitely. auth-service thread pool exhausted.
root_cause: New OAuth integration added synchronous HTTP calls (30s timeout each) inside auth-service request handler; all 200 threads blocked on I/O
resolution_steps: Check auth-service logs for 'No threads available in pool' errors
Identify synchronous HTTP calls in request handler
Rollback auth-service deployment to remove OAuth integration

alert: ALERT: order-service circuit breaker open. Checkout disabled. redis-cache metrics normal-ish.
root_cause: Redis-cache failover misconfiguration where new primary has maxmemory-policy set to 'noeviction' instead of 'allkeys-lru'; begins rejecting all writes with OOM
resolution_steps: Check redis-cache logs for 'OOM command not allowed when used memory > maxmemory'
Verify redis-cache failover state and configuration

alert: ALERT: Intermittent connection failures to all backends. 30% error rate. postgres-db timeouts.
root_cause: DNS TTL misconfiguration after load balancer IP rotation causes api-gateway to cache stale IP addresses; 30% of requests route to decommissioned hosts
resolution_steps: Check api-gateway logs for 'connection timed out: old-ip' entries
Verify DNS resolver configuration and TTL settings

alert: ALERT: notification-service processing 0 events/sec. Event queue 2M items deep. redis-cache alarming.
root_cause: Bug in order completion event handler emits 3 notification events per order instead of 1; queue grew to 2M items in 4 hours
resolution_steps: Check notification-service event processing logs for duplicate emissions
Identify event handler retry logic bug

alert: ALERT: Data inconsistency detected. Duplicate orders being created. Auth sessions stale.
root_cause: Network partition during postgres primary election resulted in two nodes both believing they are primary (split-brain); writes duplicated
red_herrings: auth-service (stale session reads causing login failures)
resolution_steps: Check postgres-db logs for split-brain indicators and conflicting transaction IDs
Run DB query to identify duplicate orders
Resolve primary election through manual intervention or failover

alert: ALERT: DB overloaded after cache flush. order-service DB connections exhausted. System slow.
root_cause: Scheduled cache flush expired all 2M cached keys simultaneously; every app server tried to rebuild cache from database causing cache stampede
red_herrings: order-service (DB connection pool exhausted from cache misses)
resolution_steps: Check redis-cache flush events in timeline
Implement gradual cache eviction or staggered invalidation

alert: ALERT: Large uploads failing with 413 errors. postgres-db write failures. 40% of API calls failing.
root_cause: New image upload feature forgotten to update api-gateway `client_max_body_size` (still at 1MB); 2-8MB images rejected with 413 errors
resolution_steps: Check api-gateway logs for 413 payload too large errors
Run DB query to verify postgres-db is healthy
Rollback api-gateway deployment to correct config

alert: ALERT: Full system degradation. p99 latency 12s. 4 services alarming. On-call paged.
root_cause: Dual simultaneous faults: (1) Missing index on postgres-db sessions table causes full scan of 80M rows per auth request; (2) Redis-cache hit memory limit with aggressive eviction causing cache thrash
resolution_steps: Run DB query on postgres-db to confirm missing index on sessions table
Check redis-cache memory usage and eviction policy
Monitor order-service CPU from retry storms
Rollback redis-cache configuration for eviction policy
Rollback postgres-db schema to recreate missing index
Declare RCA on postgres-db and redis-cache

alert: ALERT: 60% of requests failing. order-service 500s. api-gateway 502s. auth-service appears healthy.
root_cause: Service mesh sidecar (Envoy) on auth-service updated with misconfigured mTLS policy rejecting connections from order-service without new cert
resolution_steps: Check auth-service logs for mTLS rejection errors
Examine service mesh policy and certificate requirements

alert: ALERT: Intermittent 5-15s pauses across all services. SLA breached. Three services alarming.
root_cause: order-service creates 50GB/hour of short-lived objects due to JSON serialization bug allocating new byte array per field instead of streaming
resolution_steps: Check order-service GC logs for 'GC pause > 5000ms' every 8 seconds
Monitor redis-cache connection keepalive timeouts during pauses
Identify JSON serialization creating excessive objects
Profile object allocation with async profiler
Fix serialization to stream instead of allocate

alert: ALERT: Checkout for users A-F failing. 18% of users affected. order-service timeouts. DB spike.
root_cause: Redis cluster hot partition: hash slot for keys A-F (user IDs, product IDs) landed entirely on shard-2; shard-2 at 100% CPU while others idle
resolution_steps: Check redis-cache cluster status and shard CPU distribution
Identify hot partition with 100% CPU on shard-2
Analyze key distribution and hash slot allocation
Check order-service timeout errors during failures

alert: ALERT: Gradual degradation over 6 hours. Auth failing. Emails delayed. DB connections 499/500.
root_cause: Connection pool bug in ORM layer - connections not returned to pool after transaction exceptions - slowly leaking connections over 6 hours
resolution_steps: Run DB query on postgres-db to check max connections (499/500)
Query pg_stat_activity to identify idle connections with no active queries
Check for connections in 'idle' state for hours
Identify ORM exception handling not returning connections

alert: ALERT: All services slowing. api-gateway I/O wait 80%. order-service p99 8s. Disk alerts.
root_cause: Debug logging accidentally committed to production logging full request/response with base64 attachments; 40GB/hour logs fill shared disk volume
resolution_steps: Check disk I/O utilization and identify shared volume saturation
Identify debug logging of request/response bodies

alert: ALERT: API response times climbing. Queue depths alarming. System-wide slowdown over 30 min.
root_cause: order-service lacks backpressure mechanisms; downstream payment processor slow causes order-service to queue requests holding HTTP connections open
resolution_steps: Check payment processor response times (3x slower)
Check api-gateway connection pool filling with slow requests
Identify lack of backpressure in order-service

alert: ALERT: Exactly 10% of requests failing across all endpoints. Very strange pattern.
root_cause: Canary deployment of api-gateway v2.1 configured to receive 10% traffic; canary build strips Authorization header before forwarding, causing auth failures
resolution_steps: Check api-gateway logs with 'canary instance' tag on failed requests
Identify Authorization header stripping in canary build
Verify header handling differences between canary and stable

alert: ALERT: Intermittent auth failures. 25% of tokens rejected as expired. redis-cache miss rate up.
root_cause: NTP daemon killed during kernel update; auth-service clock drifted 8 minutes ahead; JWT tokens issued with future iat timestamp rejected as not yet valid
resolution_steps: Check auth-service system clock (8 minutes ahead)
Verify NTP daemon status and logs
Identify JWT tokens with future iat timestamps
Check redis-cache token cache TTL miscalculation
Restart NTP daemon and sync clock

alert: ALERT: All write operations queuing. order-service writes 30s+. System appears to be hanging.
root_cause: Database schema migration adding NOT NULL column to orders table holding ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock while backfilling 80M rows
resolution_steps: Run DB query on postgres-db to check pg_locks
Identify ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on orders table held by migration process
Kill or pause migration to release lock
Use online schema change tool (pg_repack, pt-online-schema-change)

alert: ALERT: order-service gradually degrading. File descriptor errors in logs. DB and cache timeouts.
root_cause: order-service spawns child processes for PDF generation; cleanup handler bug leaves zombies holding open file descriptors; FD limit (1024) reached
resolution_steps: Check order-service logs for EMFILE (too many open files) errors
Query system for zombie processes (ps shows 'Z' state)
Count open file descriptors in order-service process
Identify PDF generation process cleanup bug
Restart order-service to release FD resources

alert: ALERT: Massive traffic spike. api-gateway handling 10x normal load. DB overwhelmed. Origin unclear.
root_cause: Email provider 30-second outage caused 500K notification retries; retry policy had no jitter/exponential backoff; all 500K retries fired simultaneously on recovery
resolution_steps: Monitor postgres-db load from webhook confirmations
Identify email provider outage window (30 seconds)
Analyze retry policy (no jitter, no exponential backoff)
Identify simultaneous retry firing on recovery
Implement exponential backoff and jitter in retry logic

alert: ALERT: Service repeatedly restarting every 2 minutes. Pod OOMKilled. Health checks failing.
root_cause: Kubernetes pod killed by OOM (out of memory) killer; memory requests/limits misconfigured; repeated OOMKilled restarts
resolution_steps: Check Kubernetes pod status for OOMKilled restart reason
Review order-service memory limits and requests
Monitor memory usage spikes preceding OOMKilled events
Identify memory leak or resource request underestimation
Increase memory limits or fix underlying memory issue
Rollback deployment or adjust resource requests

alert: ALERT: Cross-service calls timing out after exactly 10 seconds. All traffic affected.
root_cause: Envoy sidecar proxy timeout misconfiguration causes all cross-service calls to timeout after exactly 10 seconds
resolution_steps: Check Envoy sidecar configuration on auth-service
Identify timeout policy set to exactly 10 seconds
Review timeout changes in recent sidecar updates
Check timeout metric showing exact 10s failures

alert: ALERT: Database authentication failures. All services failing to connect to postgres-db. Credential errors.
root_cause: Secrets manager rotated database credentials without notifying application; applications still using old credentials
red_herrings: auth-service (elevated error rates from connection failures)
resolution_steps: Check application logs for authentication failures
Compare database credentials in secrets manager vs cached values
Update application configuration with rotated credentials
Restart applications to pick up new secrets

alert: ALERT: Database queries degrading. Vacuum process hanging. Query performance 100x normal.
root_cause: PostgreSQL index bloat after mass deletes; vacuum stalls trying to reclaim space; queries slow significantly
resolution_steps: Run DB query to check index sizes and bloat percentages
Check query plans showing index bloat
Run manual index rebuild or reindexing
Implement regular maintenance index vacuum schedule

alert: ALERT: gRPC calls timing out. Cascading timeout failures across 3 services. Deadline exceeded errors.
root_cause: gRPC deadline propagation bug causing cascading timeout failures; deadlines not correctly propagated through service chain
resolution_steps: Check gRPC call traces for deadline exceeded errors
Verify deadline propagation through call chain
Review deadline timeout values in gRPC configuration
Identify services not respecting deadline context

alert: ALERT: Loss of metrics visibility. Logging pipeline dead. 4GB memory spike. Observability gone.
root_cause: Fluentd log aggregator consuming 4GB RAM and being OOMKilled; metrics and logs not being collected or forwarded
resolution_steps: Review Fluentd configuration for memory leaks
Restart Fluentd with higher memory limits

alert: ALERT: Service understaffed during traffic spike. Response times degrading. Autoscaler lag detected.
root_cause: Kubernetes autoscaler 5-minute lag during traffic spike leaves service understaffed for critical window
resolution_steps: Check autoscaler configuration and scaling metrics
Check HPA status and current replica count during spike

alert: ALERT: Service-to-service communication failing. mTLS errors. Certificate validation failures.
root_cause: mTLS certificate rotation left one service with expired client certificate; silently rejected by peer services

alert: ALERT: Notification queue 2M messages behind. Orders appear to vanish. Consumer lag critical.
root_cause: Kafka consumer group lag - notification-service 2M messages behind; orders not being processed or notified
resolution_steps: Check Kafka consumer group lag (2M messages)
Check consumer lag timeline and growth rate
Identify processing bottleneck in notification handler
Increase consumer parallelism or replica count

alert: ALERT: Checkout flow disabled. Feature flags not loading. Service returns all flags as OFF.
root_cause: Feature flag service outage causing all feature flags to default to OFF; checkout flow and other features disabled
resolution_steps: Check feature flag service status and logs
Verify feature flag configuration in order-service
Confirm flags defaulting to OFF state
Check feature flag service database connection

alert: ALERT: Service metrics indicate health but user reports show failures. Health check false positive.
root_cause: Health check endpoint returns 200 but service is degraded internally; readiness probe passes while handling requests fails
red_herrings: order-service (both report failures but auth looks healthy)
resolution_steps: Compare health check endpoint status vs actual request success rate
Review health check implementation and dependencies
Identify gap between health status and actual service capability
Enhance health check to validate actual request handling

alert: ALERT: Database authentication failures. All services unable to connect. Wrong database.
root_cause: Wrong database connection string in production config (staging DB URL deployed to prod); applications connecting to staging database
resolution_steps: Check database connection strings in production configuration
Identify staging database URL in production config
Confirm applications connecting to staging instead of production
Update configuration with correct production database URL

alert: ALERT: All notification consumers crashing. Poison pill detected in queue. Processing stalled.
root_cause: Malformed event in Kafka queue crashes every consumer that reads it; poison pill prevents queue processing
resolution_steps: Check notification-service consumer logs for crash stack traces
Identify malformed event in Kafka queue
Locate poison pill event in queue timeline
Skip or remove poison pill event from queue
Implement poison pill handling in consumer
Add dead-letter queue for unparseable events

alert: ALERT: New pod deployments failing. IP allocation errors. Cannot schedule new replicas.
root_cause: VPC IP address pool exhausted; new pods cannot obtain IP addresses; service replicas fail to start
resolution_steps: Check Kubernetes pod events for IP allocation failures
Query VPC IP pool exhaustion status
Count allocated IPs vs available pool

alert: ALERT: Data corruption detected. Order field values corrupted. Serialization failures.
root_cause: Dependency version conflict after library upgrade causes silent data corruption in JSON serialization
resolution_steps: Check order-service logs for serialization warnings
Compare serialized output with expected values
Downgrade problematic library to compatible version

alert: ALERT: Intermittent 15% of traffic lost. Connection failures. BGP routing anomaly.
root_cause: BGP route leak causing 15% of traffic to route to null route (blackholed); intermittent failures
resolution_steps: Check BGP routing table for anomalies
Query network border routers for BGP advertisements

alert: ALERT: Deployment failures. New pods cannot start. Resource quota hit.
root_cause: Kubernetes namespace resource quota hit; new deployments fail silently while old pods run fine
resolution_steps: Check Kubernetes ResourceQuota status in namespace
Review resource requests on failed deployments
Compare quota limits vs actual usage

alert: ALERT: Database I/O saturated. Backup job running. All queries slow. System degraded.
root_cause: Backup job running full table exports during peak hours consuming 90% of database I/O bandwidth
resolution_steps: Identify backup export job running during peak hours
Monitor backup job process and I/O consumption

alert: ALERT: User authentication rate limiting. Real user logins being rejected. Rate limit exceeded.
root_cause: Synthetic monitoring probe using production credentials causing rate limiting impacts on real users
red_herrings: api-gateway (appears to have rate limit issues)
resolution_steps: Check auth-service rate limit logs and hitting patterns
Identify synthetic monitor traffic in logs
Compare synthetic probe credentials with user credentials
Configure synthetic monitoring with separate rate-limit quota
Use dedicated monitoring credentials or IP allowlisting

alert: ALERT: Incident response delayed. MTTR (mean time to recovery) increased. Detection lag.
root_cause: New alerting rule fires too late (MTTR regression); incident already 40 minutes old before discovery
resolution_steps: Review alert firing timestamp vs actual failure timestamp
Review alerting rule configuration and thresholds
Examine alert aggregation and deduplication rules
Optimize alert sensitivity and detection delay
Tune alerting rules for faster detection
